workflow.name: AI Job Crawler with Suitability Scoring
workflow.description:
Stop reading job listings that aren't a fit. This workflow automatically crawls five major job boards (LinkedIn, Indeed, WeAreDevelopers, Stepstone, and Xing) and scores every listing against your personal applicant profile, and emails you the jobs worth your time.

An AI agent rates each job from 0 to 100 based on role, tech stack, salary, location, and deal-breakers you define. Anything below 60 gets dropped. The rest lands in your inbox, formatted and ready to review.

---

## What problem is it solving?

Job searching is time-consuming and noisy. Most listings are irrelevant, but you still have to read each one to know that. Manually filtering across five platforms for a specific stack, salary band, and working arrangement takes hours a week.

This workflow removes that friction entirely. You define your profile once and the crawler does the rest on a schedule. You get a clean digest of only the matches that pass the bar.

---

## What this workflow does

1. **Triggers** on a schedule (or manually via the Firecrawl Execute trigger)
2. **Queries** LinkedIn (via HTTP + Firecrawl), Indeed, WeAreDevelopers, Stepstone, and Xing in parallel
3. **Scrapes** each job listing with Firecrawl to extract full content
4. **Processes** posting dates with an AI agent to normalise inconsistent formats
5. **Scores** each listing 0–100 against your applicant profile using an OpenAI agent with a structured output parser
6. **Filters** out anything below 60% suitability
7. **Runs detailed research** on high-scoring jobs for additional context
8. **Sends an HTML email** with your curated job list — or a "no jobs" email if nothing passed the threshold

---

## Setup

1. **Configure your applicant profile** — edit the sticky note JSON in the workflow with your role, stack, experience, salary minimum, location preference, and deal-breakers
2. **Connect OpenAI credentials** — used for suitability scoring and date parsing
3. **Connect Firecrawl credentials** — used to scrape job board listings
4. **Configure email** — set your SMTP credentials and recipient address in both email nodes (`Job Mail` and `No Jobs Mail`)
5. **Adjust the schedule** — set how frequently the crawler runs (daily recommended)
6. **Activate the workflow**

---

## How to customize this workflow to your needs

- **Change the scoring threshold** — the filter is set to 60% by default; raise it to 75%+ for tighter results or lower it if you're early in your search
- **Add or remove job boards** — each source (LinkedIn, Indeed, etc.) is modular; you can disable boards by bypassing their query node
- **Modify the applicant profile** — update the JSON sticky note at any time to reflect a new role or changed requirements; no other changes needed
- **Adjust the detail research** — the "Detailed Research" agent runs on high-scoring jobs; you can expand or simplify what it looks for
- **Change the output** — swap the email nodes for a Notion database write, a Slack message, or a Google Sheet if you prefer a different delivery format
- **Filter by posting age** — the `max_listing_age_days` field in the profile JSON controls how recent listings must be

parameters.content: AI model comparing the found job with the applicant from 0-100, (0 does not match, 100 fully match.
Filter job that might not be that suitable. Above 60% suitability

parameters.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>

<html>
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <title>Jobs, Jobs, Jobs</title>
<style>
  .job-card {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    max-width: 800px;
    margin: 24px auto;
    padding: 24px;
    border: 1px solid #e5e7eb;
    border-radius: 16px;
    background: #ffffff;
    color: #111827;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
  }

  .job-header {
    border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb;
    padding-bottom: 16px;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
  }

  .job-title {
    margin: 0 0 8px;
    font-size: 28px;
    line-height: 1.2;
  }

  .job-subtitle {
    margin: 0;
    font-size: 15px;
    color: #6b7280;
  }

  .score-box {
    display: inline-block;
    margin-top: 14px;
    padding: 10px 14px;
    border-radius: 10px;
    background: #f3f4f6;
    font-weight: bold;
  }

  .section {
    margin-top: 24px;
  }

  .section h2 {
    margin: 0 0 12px;
    font-size: 18px;
    border-left: 4px solid #d1d5db;
    padding-left: 10px;
  }

  .grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
    gap: 12px 20px;
  }

  .field {
    padding: 12px;
    background: #f9fafb;
    border-radius: 10px;
  }

  .label {
    display: block;
    font-size: 12px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.04em;
    color: #6b7280;
    margin-bottom: 4px;
  }

  .value {
    font-size: 15px;
    font-weight: 500;
  }

  .tags {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 8px;
  }

  .tag {
    display: inline-block;
    padding: 8px 12px;
    border-radius: 999px;
    background: #eef2ff;
    color: #3730a3;
    font-size: 13px;
    font-weight: 500;
  }

  .muted {
    color: #6b7280;
  }

  .cta {
    margin-top: 28px;
    display: flex;
    gap: 12px;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }

  .button {
    display: inline-block;
    padding: 12px 18px;
    border-radius: 10px;
    text-decoration: none;
    font-weight: bold;
    background: #111827;
    color: white;
  }

  .button-secondary {
    display: inline-block;
    padding: 12px 18px;
    border-radius: 10px;
    text-decoration: none;
    font-weight: bold;
    background: #f3f4f6;
    color: #111827;
  }

  .sources {
    margin: 0;
    padding-left: 18px;
  }

  .explanation,
  .fit-text {
    line-height: 1.6;
    color: #374151;
  }

  @media (max-width: 640px) {
    .grid {
      grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    }

    .job-title {
      font-size: 22px;
    }
  }
</style>
</head>
<body>

{{ $json.output }}
  
</body>
</html>

<style>
.container {
  background-color: #ffffff;
  text-align: center;
  padding: 16px;
  border-radius: 8px;
}

h1 {
  color: #ff6d5a;
  font-size: 24px;
  font-weight: bold;
  padding: 8px;
}

h2 {
  color: #909399;
  font-size: 18px;
  font-weight: bold;
  padding: 8px;
}
</style>

<script>
</script>

parameters.jsCode:
const links = $input.first().json.data?.links || [];

return links .filter(link => typeof link === 'string' && link.includes('rc/clk?')) .map(link => ({ json: { url: link } }));

nodes.name: Crawl a website and scrape all pages in Firecrawl1

parameters.text:
=You are skilled resarch engineer. 

### OUTPUT FORMAT
PublicReview can be a review from kununu.com or trustpilot etc.

### Task 1
Please double check the provided information and try to find answers for all entries with "N/A". Be very thorough. If company name is not disclosed on the job portal write "NOT DISCLOSED", make sure to not return a job portal company or head hunter company when the job is not explicitly working for that company but instead is only conveyed by them.

### Task 2
Funding infromation might be a recent news regarding the company name.

### Task 3
If salary range is not defined so far, try to find this infromation of the same job listing on the company career page. Job has to match exactly. When in doubt return "N/A"

### Task 4
If name of the company is known, try to find out how many employees are employed in this company.
Source might be wikipedia or the company homepage.

### WEB SEARCH
Utilize the firecrawl tool to research positions on pages. Add every visited paged to SourceLinks in the output json.

### JOB DESCRIPTION
{{ JSON.stringify($('Loop over all jobs').item.json, null, 2) }}

parameters.html:
{{ (() => { const job = $("Detailled Research").first().json.output; const rating = $("Rate suitablility").item.json.output || {}; const stackHtml = Array.isArray(job.Stack) && job.Stack.length ? job.Stack.map(skill => `${skill}`).join(" - ") : `N/A`; const sourcesHtml = Array.isArray(job.SourceLinks) && job.SourceLinks.length ? job.SourceLinks.map((link, index) => `
  • Source ${index + 1}
    • ` ).join("") : `
  • No sources available
    • `; return `

    ${job.Role || "Untitled role"}

    Company: ${job.Company || "Company name not specified"}

    Location: ${job.JobLocation || "Location not specified"}

    Match score: ${rating.Score ?? "N/A"}

    Description

    ${job.JobDescription || "No description available. What a lacy AI..."}

    Assessment

    ${rating.Explanation || "No explanation available."}

    Job details

    Salary range: ${job.SalaryRange || "N/A"}
    Posted: ${job.Posted || "N/A"}
    Company size: ${job.CompanySize || "N/A"}
    Funding: ${job.Funding || "N/A"}
    Public review: ${job.PublicReview || "N/A"}

    Why it fits

    ${job.WhyItFits || "No fit summary available."}

    Tech stack

    ${stackHtml}

    Sources

      ${sourcesHtml}
    Apply now
    `; })() }}

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
"Company": "Company name",
"CompanyLocation": "Company location",
"Role": "Role",
"JobLocation": "Job loation",
"JobDescription": "Job Description",
"SalaryRange": "Salery Range",
"Posted": "Date",
"Stack": ["Tech Stack 1","Tech Stack 2"],
"CompanySize": "company size",
"Funding": "Funding Type",
"PublicReview": "Review Signal",
"WhyItFits": "Description",
"Link": "URL",
"SourceLinks": ["URL1", "URL2"]
}

parameters.text:
=#### Task 1
Bring this date "{{ $json.data.json.Posted }}" in the format YYYY-MM-DD (today is {{ $now }}) if possible.

#### Task 2
Convert the Salery Range "{{ $json.data.json.SalaryRange }}" to € (p.A) with grouping dots. Call the firecrawl tool if you need recent exchange rates. Add original value in brakets () after that value. If not possible return null. Use Websearch if you need clarifications for "Entgeldgruppen" etc.

### Clarification

Always output the json, use the value null if no information can be found

{
"Posted": "Date",
"SalaryRange": "Salary"
}

parameters.toolDescription:
Call this function to get current exchange rates.
URL has the form: https://www.xe.com/currencyconverter/convert/?Amount=118000&From=CHF&To=EUR

parameters.jsCode:
const links = $input.first().json.data?.links || [];

return links .filter(link => typeof link === 'string' && link.includes('xing.com/jobs')) .map(link => ({ json: { url: link } }));

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert researcher. Extract all needed information from the source.
Posted Date might be in the form of "x days ago" (vor x Tagen).
If no information can be found write "N/A". Do not hallucinate/invent infromation.

{
"Company": "The company name. Do not add if the mentioned company is a job placement or headhunter agency.",
"CompanyLocation": "The Company location.",
"Role": "The Job Role",
"JobLocation": "The Job loation. Might be remote, if it is a remote position.",
"JobDescription": "The complete Job Description.",
"SalaryRange": "The Salery Range for this job posting.",
"Posted": "The date the job posting was published. Might be in the form of x days ago (vor x Tagen)",
"Stack": ["The tech stack that this jobs requieres","Tech Stack 2"],
"CompanySize": "The amount of employees in this company."
}

  • Source ${index + 1}
    • ` ).join("") : `
  • No sources available
    • `; return `

    ${job.Role || "Untitled role"}

      ${sourcesHtml}
    Apply now
    `; })() }}

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
